refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-003 Refined: The NGHE solution shall enable operators and tele-operators to achieve a measured energy efficiency of energy_per_ton ≤ E_target (customer-defined threshold) over the agreed evaluation period, through integrated monitoring, control optimization, and real-time feedback mechanisms.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is outcome-focused but lacks explicit linkage to the mechanisms (monitoring, control, feedback) that enable efficiency achievement. Refinement clarifies that energy efficiency is not a passive property but an active capability requiring measurement, optimization logic, and operator awareness. This supports both direct operation and teleoperation modes.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure energy_per_ton = total_energy_consumed / total_tonnage_moved over a defined evaluation period (e.g., 40-hour work cycle). Verify that measured value ≤ E_target. Collect telemetry from Vehicle power systems and cross-validate with site energy meters. Confirm operator feedback loops are functional.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-003.1: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and log energy consumption (kWh or equivalent) from all propulsion, hydraulic, and auxiliary systems.
CR-NGHE-003.2: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and log work output (tonnage moved, distance traveled, or equivalent productivity metric).
CR-NGHE-003.3: The NGHE solution shall compute and display real-time energy efficiency (energy_per_ton) to the operator or tele-operator.
CR-NGHE-003.4: The NGHE solution shall provide control recommendations or automated optimization to reduce energy_per_ton below E_target.
CR-NGHE-003.5: The NGHE solution shall log all energy and productivity data for post-evaluation analysis and reporting.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement a centralized energy management system (EMS) that aggregates power telemetry from Vehicle subsystems, computes efficiency metrics in real-time, and broadcasts optimization commands to propulsion and hydraulic controllers.
DP-B: Distribute energy monitoring across Vehicle subsystems with local efficiency computation; aggregate results at the Teleoperation gateway for operator display and feedback.

DP-C: Implement a hybrid approach: Vehicle-level power measurement and local optimization logic, with Teleoperation-level aggregation, reporting, and operator guidance; Safety Services validates efficiency compliance against E_target thresholds.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Hybrid energy management with Vehicle-level measurement and optimization, Teleoperation-level aggregation and operator guidance, and Safety Services compliance validation. This approach distributes responsibility appropriately: the Vehicle owns power measurement and local control optimization; Teleoperation owns operator-facing efficiency feedback and remote guidance; Safety Services owns threshold compliance monitoring and alerting.

potential_sources: Telemetry interface coupling: Vehicle must emit energy and productivity data; Teleoperation must consume and process it. Coupling is necessary and well-defined.
Tasking interface coupling: Teleoperation may issue efficiency-optimization commands (e.g., 'reduce idle time', 'optimize bucket fill cycle') to Vehicle. Coupling is intentional.
SafetyAlert coupling: Safety Services monitors energy_per_ton against E_target and may issue alerts if threshold is breached. Coupling is one-way (Safety Services → Operator).
coupling_check.notes: DP-C exhibits intentional, well-scoped couplings across three modules. No circular dependencies detected. Coupling is mediated by well-defined interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert), reducing risk of hidden dependencies.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Vehicle power measurement subsystems are accurate and calibrated. Risk: Measurement drift or sensor failure could invalidate efficiency calculations. Mitigation: Implement sensor redundancy and periodic calibration checks.

Assumption: Teleoperation system has sufficient bandwidth and latency performance to receive Telemetry and issue Tasking commands in real-time. Risk: Network congestion could delay feedback, reducing operator responsiveness. Mitigation: Prioritize efficiency-related telemetry; implement local Vehicle-level optimization as fallback.

Assumption: Safety Services can reliably compute and monitor E_target compliance. Risk: Ambiguity in E_target definition (e.g., rolling average vs. instantaneous) could cause false alerts. Mitigation: Establish clear E_target specification and validation criteria upfront.

Assumption: Operator behavior and site conditions (terrain, load, weather) are within expected ranges. Risk: Extreme conditions could make E_target unachievable. Mitigation: Define E_target with site-specific adjustment factors; document boundary conditions.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the role of Safety Services in DP-C: is it purely monitoring/alerting, or does it have authority to restrict operations if E_target is breached? Define the escalation path (e.g., alert → operator acknowledgment → forced shutdown if threshold exceeded). This will refine the SafetyAlert interface semantics.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level system boundary that integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules. At this level, CR-NGHE-003 energy efficiency capability is realized through orchestration of energy measurement (Vehicle), operator guidance (Teleoperation), and compliance monitoring (Safety Services). The NGHE Solution owns the end-to-end energy efficiency KPI and is responsible for ensuring energy_per_ton ≤ E_target.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits energy consumption (kWh), productivity metrics (tonnage, distance), and computed energy_per_ton to external systems (e.g., site management, customer dashboards). Includes Vehicle power data and efficiency status.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives operational commands and efficiency optimization directives from site managers or tele-operators. Includes work assignments, efficiency targets, and mode changes (e.g., 'eco mode', 'standard mode').

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits safety and compliance alerts, including energy efficiency threshold breaches (e.g., 'energy_per_ton exceeds E_target by 15%'). Directed to Safety Manager and site personnel.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution communicates with remote tele-operators and cloud-based monitoring systems via Network Link. Carries Telemetry, Tasking, and SafetyAlert payloads.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for CR-NGHE-003. It owns the end-to-end energy efficiency KPI, orchestrates sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services), and is responsible for verification. Minimal diffusion: energy efficiency logic is distributed across Vehicle (measurement/optimization), Teleoperation (operator guidance), and Safety Services (compliance), but NGHE Solution owns the integration and accountability.

Vehicle owns power measurement and local optimization logic (sub-FRs 003.1, 003.4 partially). However, operator-facing efficiency display (003.3) and compliance reporting (003.5) are outside Vehicle scope. Significant diffusion: energy efficiency capability is split between Vehicle and Teleoperation/Safety Services.

Teleoperation owns operator guidance and efficiency feedback (sub-FR 003.3 partially). However, it does not own power measurement (Vehicle) or compliance monitoring (Safety Services). Moderate containment: Teleoperation is a consumer and presenter of efficiency data, not a primary owner.

Safety Services owns compliance monitoring and alerting (sub-FR 003.5 partially, SafetyAlert). However, it does not own measurement or operator guidance. Low containment: Safety Services is a secondary stakeholder in energy efficiency, focused on threshold enforcement rather than primary capability delivery.

assigned_im.ownership_candidate: NGHE Solution (primary owner, with delegated responsibilities to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services)
assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: CR-NGHE-003 energy efficiency capability is best realized as a distributed, orchestrated system spanning three sub-modules: (1) Vehicle owns power measurement and local optimization; (2) Teleoperation owns operator-facing efficiency feedback and remote guidance; (3) Safety Services owns compliance monitoring and alerting. NGHE Solution integrates these three and owns the end-to-end KPI. This distribution aligns with natural module boundaries and stakeholder responsibilities (Equipment Operator, Tele-operator, Safety Manager).
containment_risks: Risk: Diffusion of energy efficiency logic across three modules could lead to inconsistent efficiency calculations or conflicting optimization commands. Mitigation: Establish a canonical energy_per_ton computation formula and enforce it across all modules. Define clear ownership of optimization logic (e.g., Vehicle owns local control, Teleoperation owns operator guidance, Safety Services owns threshold enforcement).

Risk: Telemetry interface may become a bottleneck if Vehicle emits high-frequency power data. Mitigation: Implement data aggregation and filtering at Vehicle level; emit only summary statistics and alerts to Teleoperation.

Risk: Safety Services compliance monitoring may lag behind real-time Vehicle operations, leading to delayed alerts. Mitigation: Implement real-time threshold checking at Vehicle level; Safety Services performs post-hoc validation and reporting.

Risk: Operator behavior (e.g., ignoring efficiency guidance) could undermine the capability. Mitigation: Implement incentive mechanisms (e.g., efficiency bonuses, performance dashboards) and training to encourage operator engagement.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Refine the interface contracts between NGHE Solution sub-modules: (1) Define the Telemetry payload schema for energy and productivity data (frequency, precision, units). (2) Define the Tasking payload schema for efficiency optimization commands (e.g., 'reduce idle time by 10%', 'switch to eco mode'). (3) Define the SafetyAlert payload schema for threshold breaches (e.g., 'energy_per_ton = 2.5 kWh/ton, E_target = 2.0 kWh/ton, breach = 25%'). (4) Establish a Service Level Agreement (SLA) for Telemetry latency and SafetyAlert response time.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-C (hybrid energy management) directly addresses CR-NGHE-003 refined FR. The DP specifies a clear solution approach: Vehicle-level measurement and optimization, Teleoperation-level aggregation and operator guidance, Safety Services-level compliance monitoring. The DP is traceable to all sub-FRs (003.1–003.5) and supports the verification idea (measure energy_per_ton over evaluation period). No independence axiom violations detected: each sub-FR is addressed by a distinct DP component.

diffusion_risks: Energy efficiency logic is distributed across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Risk: Inconsistent implementations or conflicting optimization commands. Mitigation: Establish canonical formulas and clear ownership boundaries.

Telemetry interface carries energy and productivity data. Risk: High-frequency data could overwhelm Teleoperation or Network Link. Mitigation: Implement data aggregation and filtering at Vehicle level.

SafetyAlert interface carries compliance alerts. Risk: Delayed alerts could reduce safety effectiveness. Mitigation: Implement real-time threshold checking at Vehicle level; Safety Services performs post-hoc validation.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate top-level container. Energy efficiency capability is realized through orchestration of Vehicle (measurement/optimization), Teleoperation (operator guidance), and Safety Services (compliance monitoring). Containment score of 95 for NGHE Solution reflects strong alignment with the FR. Diffusion is intentional and well-scoped across three sub-modules with clear interface contracts.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: CR-NGHE-003 is verifiable at the NGHE Solution level. Verification approach: (1) Measure energy_per_ton over a defined evaluation period (e.g., 40-hour work cycle). (2) Collect telemetry from Vehicle power systems and productivity metrics. (3) Compute energy_per_ton = total_energy / total_tonnage. (4) Compare against E_target. (5) Validate operator feedback loops and Safety Services alerts. Feasibility is high; measurement infrastructure is standard in heavy equipment.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of CR-NGHE-003 to NGHE Solution is clear. Sub-allocations to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are well-defined via sub-FRs (003.1–003.5). Ownership boundaries are explicit: Vehicle owns measurement/optimization, Teleoperation owns operator guidance, Safety Services owns compliance monitoring. Stakeholder responsibilities are aligned: Equipment Operator and Tele-operator interact with Telemetry/Tasking; Safety Manager monitors SafetyAlert.

recommended_revisions: Establish a detailed interface specification document (ICD) for Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, and Network Link. Include payload schemas, data types, frequencies, and latency requirements.
Define the E_target specification: is it a fixed value, a rolling average, or site-specific? Establish acceptance criteria and boundary conditions (e.g., extreme terrain, overload scenarios).
Clarify the role of Safety Services in DP-C: is it purely monitoring/alerting, or does it have authority to restrict operations? Define escalation paths and operator override procedures.
Establish a test plan for CR-NGHE-003 verification, including measurement methodology, evaluation period, and acceptance criteria. Include both direct operation and teleoperation modes.
Document assumptions and risks (e.g., sensor accuracy, network latency, operator behavior) and establish mitigation strategies.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-003 refined FR is well-defined and traceable to DP-C and NGHE Solution IM. However, the FR is not atomic: it comprises five sub-FRs (003.1–003.5) that require further decomposition. Each sub-FR addresses a distinct functional aspect (measurement, productivity tracking, efficiency display, optimization, reporting) and should be decomposed to the next level to identify specific DPs and IMs. Proceeding with ZIGZAG_DOWN allows systematic refinement of each sub-FR and ensures comprehensive coverage of the energy efficiency capability.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-003.1: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and log energy consumption (kWh or equivalent) from all propulsion, hydraulic, and auxiliary systems.
CR-NGHE-003.2: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and log work output (tonnage moved, distance traveled, or equivalent productivity metric).
CR-NGHE-003.3: The NGHE solution shall compute and display real-time energy efficiency (energy_per_ton) to the operator or tele-operator.
CR-NGHE-003.4: The NGHE solution shall provide control recommendations or automated optimization to reduce energy_per_ton below E_target.
CR-NGHE-003.5: The NGHE solution shall log all energy and productivity data for post-evaluation analysis and reporting.

assumptions: Vehicle power measurement subsystems are accurate and calibrated.
Teleoperation system has sufficient bandwidth and latency performance for real-time efficiency feedback.
Safety Services can reliably compute and monitor E_target compliance.
Operator behavior and site conditions are within expected ranges.
E_target is defined with clear acceptance criteria and boundary conditions.
Energy efficiency is a measurable, controllable system property (not purely dependent on external factors).
open_risks: Measurement drift or sensor failure could invalidate efficiency calculations.
Network congestion could delay efficiency feedback, reducing operator responsiveness.
Ambiguity in E_target definition could cause false alerts or inconsistent compliance assessment.
Extreme site conditions (terrain, load, weather) could make E_target unachievable.
Operator behavior (e.g., ignoring efficiency guidance) could undermine the capability.
Diffusion of energy efficiency logic across three modules could lead to inconsistent implementations.
High-frequency Telemetry data could overwhelm Teleoperation or Network Link.
Delayed SafetyAlert could reduce safety effectiveness.
evidence_needed: Detailed interface specification (ICD) for Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link.
E_target specification document with acceptance criteria and boundary conditions.
Safety Services role and authority definition (monitoring vs. enforcement).
Test plan for CR-NGHE-003 verification, including measurement methodology and evaluation period.
Sensor accuracy and calibration specifications for Vehicle power measurement.
Network latency and bandwidth requirements for Teleoperation efficiency feedback.
Operator training and incentive program design for efficiency engagement.
Site-specific adjustment factors for E_target (terrain, load, weather).
